title: Coelogyne integrilabia
intro: Coelogyne integrilabia is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne integrilabia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coelogyne integrilabia include teas and extracts, which are used to alleviate various health issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Coelogyne integrilabia is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves, which are typical of the Orchidaceae family. Specifically, it belongs to the genus Coelogyne, a group of terrestrial orchids. Within the Coelogyne genus, C. integrilabia is further classified based on its unique features, such as its white and purple flowers. The classification of C. integrilabia provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other orchid species.
uses: Coelogyne integrilabia. Research on the medicinal uses of Coelogyne integrilabia is limited, but some studies suggest that it may have antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and effects of Coelogyne integrilabia. Some studies also indicate its potential use in treating wounds and infections.
constituents: Coelogyne integrilabia contains iridoids and other compounds.  However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's active constituents.
